content:
🔥 textream | Swift
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a discreet and customizable teleprompter experience for macOS users during live streams, interviews, or presentations.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time word tracking using on-device speech recognition • Classic auto-scroll with adjustable speed • Voice-activated scrolling that pauses when silent • Dynamic Island-style overlay, draggable floating window, or fullscreen on Sidecar iPad
📖 Summary: Textream is a free, open-source macOS teleprompter application designed for streamers, interviewers, and presenters. It offers real-time word tracking, classic auto-scroll, and voice-activated scrolling, displayed in a discreet overlay that is invisible to the audience.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ maxun | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling users to extract structured data from websites and turn them into APIs through a no-code interface.
✨ Key Features: • No-code web data platform • Real-time web scraping and crawling • AI-powered data extraction • Recorder Mode for robot creation • SDK for developers
📖 Summary: Maxun is an open-source, no-code platform designed to transform websites into structured data APIs. It offers tools for web scraping, crawling, search, and AI-powered data extraction, enabling users to automate data collection without writing code.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ telegram-mcp | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To act as a bridge between AI models/clients and the Telegram messaging platform, enabling AI to read, understand, and interact with Telegram chats and groups programmatically.
✨ Key Features: • Full-featured Telegram integration for MCP-compatible clients (Claude, Cursor, etc.) • Powered by Telethon and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) • Programmatic interaction with Telegram accounts
📖 Summary: Telegram MCP Server is a Python-based server that leverages the Telethon library to provide a comprehensive interface for interacting with Telegram. It allows MCP-compatible clients, such as AI assistants, to read chats, manage groups, send and modify messages, media, contacts, and settings.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 clipboard-history | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Managing and searching clipboard history on Linux systems.
✨ Key Features: • Remembers clipboard history across reboots • Saves images and arbitrary bytes • Searchable history with Regex or raw queries • Installable on any Linux system • Efficient storage using custom binary format
📖 Summary: Ringboard is a Rust-based clipboard manager for Linux that efficiently stores, searches, and retrieves clipboard history, including images and arbitrary data. It features a client-server architecture for composability and supports a large history size with minimal resource usage.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 noctalia-shell | QML
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a beautiful, minimal, and highly customizable desktop shell experience for Wayland users.
✨ Key Features: • Native support for Niri, Hyprland, Sway, Scroll, Labwc and MangoWC • Extensive theming with predefined color schemes and automatic color generation from your wallpaper • Wallpaper management with Wallhaven integration • Notification system with history and Do Not Disturb • Multi-monitor support
📖 Summary: Noctalia Shell is a minimal and customizable desktop shell for Wayland, built on Quickshell (Qt/QML). It offers extensive theming, wallpaper management, a notification system, multi-monitor support, and a lock screen, with nearly 100 available plugins.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 gdrive | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Managing Google Drive files and folders via a command-line interface.
✨ Key Features: • Command-line interface for Google Drive interaction • File and folder management • Account management (add, export, import) • Successor to gdrive2 with basic functionality implemented
📖 Summary: gdrive is a command-line interface (CLI) application built with Rust for interacting with Google Drive. It allows users to manage their files and folders directly from the terminal, acting as a successor to previous gdrive versions.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
